description: The composite building material in which aggregate is held by a binder that hardens is 'kapenu'. Cement is one possible binder rather than another name for the whole material. A construction record supplies the mixture and curing history, along with any reinforcement or rating that the work depends on.
articulatory_notes: A soft-palate closure releases unvoiced 'k' into open 'a'. The lips meet for stressed unvoiced 'p' and open into front 'e'; dental nasal 'n' finishes with rounded high-back 'u'.
usage_notes: Concrete needs no classifier as poured material. A concrete object may be counted with optional 'themo'; an engineering account can then identify its age, condition, and test results.

pillars.solarpunk-values: A concrete wall begins with quarried aggregate, binder, water, and labour brought from elsewhere. That history matters when people decide whether to repair it, reuse it, or replace it.

semantic_domains.creation: a composite material placed and hardened in buildings and infrastructure
semantic_domains.physical: aggregate held within a hardened binder
